fields.name: servicenetworking_operations_list
fields.description: Lists operations that match the specified filter in the request. If the server doesn't support this method, it returns `UNIMPLEMENTED`.

args.name: returnPartialSuccess
args.description: When set to `true`, operations that are reachable are returned as normal, and those that are unreachable are returned in the ListOperationsResponse.unreachable field. This can only be `true` when reading across collections. For example, when `parent` is set to `"projects/example/locations/-"`. This field is not supported by default and will result in an `UNIMPLEMENTED` error if set unless explicitly documented otherwise in service or product specific documentation.

fields.name: servicenetworking_operations_get
fields.description: Gets the latest state of a long-running operation. Clients can use this method to poll the operation result at intervals as recommended by the API service.

fields.name: servicenetworking_services_connections_list
fields.description: List the private connections that are configured in a service consumer's VPC network.

args.name: network
args.description: Required. The name of service consumer's VPC network that's connected with service producer network through a private connection. The network name must be in the following format: `projects/{project}/global/networks/{network}`. {project} is a project number, such as in `12345` that includes the VPC service consumer's VPC network. {network} is the name of the service consumer's VPC network.

fields.name: servicenetworking_services_projects_global_networks_getVpcServiceControls
fields.description: Consumers use this method to find out the state of VPC Service Controls. The controls could be enabled or disabled for a connection.

fields.name: servicenetworking_services_projects_global_networks_get
fields.description: Service producers use this method to get the configuration of their connection including the import/export of custom routes and subnetwork routes with public IP.

args.name: includeUsedIpRanges
args.description: Optional. When true, include the used IP ranges as part of the GetConsumerConfig output. This includes routes created inside the service networking network, consumer network, peers of the consumer network, and reserved ranges inside the service networking network. By default, this is false

fields.name: servicenetworking_services_projects_global_networks_dnsZones_list
fields.description: * Service producers can use this method to retrieve a list of available DNS zones in the shared producer host project and the matching peering zones in the consumer project. *

fields.name: servicenetworking_services_projects_global_networks_dnsZones_get
fields.description: Service producers can use this method to retrieve a DNS zone in the shared producer host project and the matching peering zones in consumer project

fields.name: servicenetworking_services_projects_global_networks_peeredDnsDomains_list
fields.description: Lists peered DNS domains for a connection.

fields.name: servicenetworking_services_dnsRecordSets_get
fields.description: Producers can use this method to retrieve information about the DNS record set added to the private zone inside the shared tenant host project associated with a consumer network.

args.name: consumerNetwork
args.description: Required. The consumer network containing the record set. Must be in the form of projects/{project}/global/networks/{network}

args.name: zone
args.description: Required. The name of the zone containing the record set.

args.name: domain
args.description: Required. The domain name of the zone containing the recordset.

args.name: type
args.description: Required. RecordSet Type eg. type='A'. See the list of [Supported DNS Types](https://cloud.google.com/dns/records/json-record).

fields.name: servicenetworking_services_dnsRecordSets_list
fields.description: Producers can use this method to retrieve a list of available DNS RecordSets available inside the private zone on the tenant host project accessible from their network.

args.name: consumerNetwork
args.description: Required. The network that the consumer is using to connect with services. Must be in the form of projects/{project}/global/networks/{network} {project} is the project number, as in '12345' {network} is the network name.

args.name: zone
args.description: Required. The name of the private DNS zone in the shared producer host project from which the record set will be removed.

types.name: Int
types.description: The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.

types.name: ListOperationsResponse
types.description: The response message for Operations.ListOperations.

fields.name: operations
fields.description: A list of operations that matches the specified filter in the request.

fields.name: nextPageToken
fields.description: The standard List next-page token.

fields.name: unreachable
fields.description: Unordered list. Unreachable resources. Populated when the request sets `ListOperationsRequest.return_partial_success` and reads across collections. For example, when attempting to list all resources across all supported locations.

types.name: Operation
types.description: This resource represents a long-running operation that is the result of a network API call.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The server-assigned name, which is only unique within the same service that originally returns it. If you use the default HTTP mapping, the `name` should be a resource name ending with `operations/{unique_id}`.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Service-specific metadata associated with the operation. It typically contains progress information and common metadata such as create time. Some services might not provide such metadata. Any method that returns a long-running operation should document the metadata type, if any.

fields.name: done
fields.description: If the value is `false`, it means the operation is still in progress. If `true`, the operation is completed, and either `error` or `response` is available.

fields.name: response
fields.description: The normal, successful response of the operation. If the original method returns no data on success, such as `Delete`, the response is `google.protobuf.Empty`. If the original method is standard `Get`/`Create`/`Update`, the response should be the resource. For other methods, the response should have the type `XxxResponse`, where `Xxx` is the original method name. For example, if the original method name is `TakeSnapshot()`, the inferred response type is `TakeSnapshotResponse`.

types.name: JSON
types.description: The `JSON` scalar type represents JSON values as specified by [ECMA-404](http://www.ecma-international.org/publications/files/ECMA-ST/ECMA-404.pdf).

types.name: Status
types.description: The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each `Status` message contains three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details. You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the [API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

fields.name: code
fields.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of google.rpc.Code.

fields.name: message
fields.description: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the google.rpc.Status.details field, or localized by the client.

fields.name: details
fields.description: A list of messages that carry the error details. There is a common set of message types for APIs to use.

types.name: ListConnectionsResponse
types.description: ListConnectionsResponse is the response to list peering states for the given service and consumer project.

fields.name: connections
fields.description: The list of Connections.

types.name: Connection
types.description: Represents a private connection resource. A private connection is implemented as a VPC Network Peering connection between a service producer's VPC network and a service consumer's VPC network.

fields.name: network
fields.description: Required. The name of service consumer's VPC network that's connected with service producer network, in the following format: `projects/{project}/global/networks/{network}`. `{project}` is a project number, such as in `12345` that includes the VPC service consumer's VPC network. `{network}` is the name of the service consumer's VPC network.

fields.name: reservedPeeringRanges
fields.description: The name of one or more allocated IP address ranges for this service producer of type `PEERING`. Note that invoking CreateConnection method with a different range when connection is already established will not modify already provisioned service producer subnetworks. If CreateConnection method is invoked repeatedly to reconnect when peering connection had been disconnected on the consumer side, leaving this field empty will restore previously allocated IP ranges.

fields.name: peering
fields.description: Output only. The name of the VPC Network Peering connection that was created by the service producer.

fields.name: service
fields.description: Output only. The name of the peering service that's associated with this connection, in the following format: `services/{service name}`.

types.name: VpcServiceControls
types.description: Response for the get VPC Service Controls request.

fields.name: enabled
fields.description: Output only. Indicates whether the VPC Service Controls are enabled or disabled for the connection. If the consumer called the EnableVpcServiceControls method, then this is true. If the consumer called DisableVpcServiceControls, then this is false. The default is false.

types.name: ConsumerConfig
types.description: Configuration information for a private service access connection.

fields.name: producerNetwork
fields.description: Output only. The VPC host network that is used to host managed service instances. In the format, projects/{project}/global/networks/{network} where {project} is the project number e.g. '12345' and {network} is the network name.

fields.name: consumerImportCustomRoutes
fields.description: Import custom routes flag value for peering from consumer to producer.

fields.name: consumerExportCustomRoutes
fields.description: Export custom routes flag value for peering from consumer to producer.

fields.name: producerImportCustomRoutes
fields.description: Import custom routes flag value for peering from producer to consumer.

fields.name: producerExportCustomRoutes
fields.description: Export custom routes flag value for peering from producer to consumer.

fields.name: consumerImportSubnetRoutesWithPublicIp
fields.description: Import subnet routes with public ip flag value for peering from consumer to producer.

fields.name: consumerExportSubnetRoutesWithPublicIp
fields.description: Export subnet routes with public ip flag value for peering from consumer to producer.

fields.name: producerImportSubnetRoutesWithPublicIp
fields.description: Import subnet routes with public ip flag value for peering from producer to consumer.

fields.name: producerExportSubnetRoutesWithPublicIp
fields.description: Export subnet routes with public ip flag value for peering from producer to consumer.

fields.name: reservedRanges
fields.description: Output only. The reserved ranges associated with this private service access connection.

fields.name: vpcScReferenceArchitectureEnabled
fields.description: Output only. Indicates whether the VPC Service Controls reference architecture is configured for the producer VPC host network.

fields.name: usedIpRanges
fields.description: Output only. The IP ranges already in use by consumer or producer

fields.name: cloudsqlConfigs
fields.description: Represents one or multiple Cloud SQL configurations.

fields.name: consumerPeeringActive
fields.description: Output only. If this is true, consumer peering is active.

types.name: GoogleCloudServicenetworkingV1ConsumerConfigReservedRange
types.description: Allocated IP address ranges for this private service access connection.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the reserved range.

fields.name: ipPrefixLength
fields.description: The prefix length of the reserved range.

fields.name: address
fields.description: The starting address of the reserved range. The address must be a valid IPv4 address in the x.x.x.x format. This value combined with the IP prefix length is the CIDR range for the reserved range.

types.name: CloudSQLConfig
types.description: Cloud SQL configuration.

fields.name: service
fields.description: Required. Peering service used for peering with the Cloud SQL project.

fields.name: umbrellaProject
fields.description: Required. The project number of the Cloud SQL umbrella project.

fields.name: umbrellaNetwork
fields.description: Required. The name of the umbrella network in the Cloud SQL umbrella project.

types.name: BigInt
types.description: The `BigInt` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values.

types.name: ListDnsZonesResponse
types.description: Represents all DNS zones in the shared producer host project and the matching peering zones in the consumer project.

fields.name: dnsZonePairs
fields.description: All pairs of private DNS zones in the shared producer host project and the matching peering zones in the consumer project..

types.name: DnsZonePair
types.description: * Represents a pair of private and peering DNS zone resources. *

types.name: DnsZone
types.description: Represents a DNS zone resource.

fields.name: name
fields.description: User assigned name for this resource. Must be unique within the project. The name must be 1-63 characters long, must begin with a letter, end with a letter or digit, and only contain lowercase letters, digits or dashes.

fields.name: dnsSuffix
fields.description: The DNS name suffix of this zone e.g. `example.com.`. Cloud DNS requires that a DNS suffix ends with a trailing dot.

types.name: GetDnsZoneResponse
types.description: Represents managed DNS zones created in the shared Producer host and consumer projects.

types.name: ListPeeredDnsDomainsResponse
types.description: Response to list peered DNS domains for a given connection.

fields.name: peeredDnsDomains
fields.description: The list of peered DNS domains.

types.name: PeeredDnsDomain
types.description: DNS domain suffix for which requests originating in the producer VPC network are resolved in the associated consumer VPC network.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Required. User assigned name for this resource. Must be unique within the consumer network. The name must be 1-63 characters long, must begin with a letter, end with a letter or digit, and only contain lowercase letters, digits or dashes.

fields.name: dnsSuffix
fields.description: The DNS domain name suffix e.g. `example.com.`. Cloud DNS requires that a DNS suffix ends with a trailing dot.

types.name: DnsRecordSet
types.description: Represents a DNS record set resource.

fields.name: domain
fields.description: Required. The DNS or domain name of the record set, e.g. `test.example.com`. Cloud DNS requires that a DNS suffix ends with a trailing dot.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Required. The identifier of a supported record type.

fields.name: ttl
fields.description: Required. The period of time for which this RecordSet can be cached by resolvers.

fields.name: data
fields.description: Required. As defined in RFC 1035 (section 5) and RFC 1034 (section 3.6.1) for examples see https://cloud.google.com/dns/records/json-record.

types.name: ListDnsRecordSetsResponse
types.description: Represents all DNS RecordSets associated with the producer network

fields.name: dnsRecordSets
fields.description: DNS record Set Resource

fields.name: servicenetworking_operations_delete
fields.description: Deletes a long-running operation. This method indicates that the client is no longer interested in the operation result. It does not cancel the operation. If the server doesn't support this method, it returns `google.rpc.Code.UNIMPLEMENTED`.

fields.name: servicenetworking_operations_cancel
fields.description: Starts asynchronous cancellation on a long-running operation. The server makes a best effort to cancel the operation, but success is not guaranteed. If the server doesn't support this method, it returns `google.rpc.Code.UNIMPLEMENTED`. Clients can use Operations.GetOperation or other methods to check whether the cancellation succeeded or whether the operation completed despite cancellation. On successful cancellation, the operation is not deleted; instead, it becomes an operation with an Operation.error value with a google.rpc.Status.code of `1`, corresponding to `Code.CANCELLED`.

fields.name: servicenetworking_services_disableVpcServiceControls
fields.description: Disables VPC service controls for a connection.

fields.name: servicenetworking_services_enableVpcServiceControls
fields.description: Enables VPC service controls for a connection.

fields.name: servicenetworking_services_addSubnetwork
fields.description: For service producers, provisions a new subnet in a peered service's shared VPC network in the requested region and with the requested size that's expressed as a CIDR range (number of leading bits of ipV4 network mask). The method checks against the assigned allocated ranges to find a non-conflicting IP address range. The method will reuse a subnet if subsequent calls contain the same subnet name, region, and prefix length. This method will make producer's tenant project to be a shared VPC service project as needed.

fields.name: servicenetworking_services_searchRange
fields.description: Service producers can use this method to find a currently unused range within consumer allocated ranges. This returned range is not reserved, and not guaranteed to remain unused. It will validate previously provided allocated ranges, find non-conflicting sub-range of requested size (expressed in number of leading bits of ipv4 network mask, as in CIDR range notation).

fields.name: servicenetworking_services_validate
fields.description: Service producers use this method to validate if the consumer provided network, project and requested range are valid. This allows them to use a fail-fast mechanism for consumer requests, and not have to wait for AddSubnetwork operation completion to determine if user request is invalid.

fields.name: servicenetworking_services_connections_create
fields.description: Creates a private connection that establishes a VPC Network Peering connection to a VPC network in the service producer's organization. The administrator of the service consumer's VPC network invokes this method. The administrator must assign one or more allocated IP ranges for provisioning subnetworks in the service producer's VPC network. This connection is used for all supported services in the service producer's organization, so it only needs to be invoked once.

fields.name: servicenetworking_services_connections_deleteConnection
fields.description: Deletes a private service access connection.

fields.name: servicenetworking_services_connections_patch
fields.description: Updates the allocated ranges that are assigned to a connection.

args.name: updateMask
args.description: The update mask. If this is omitted, it defaults to "*". You can only update the listed peering ranges.

args.name: force
args.description: If a previously defined allocated range is removed, force flag must be set to true.

fields.name: servicenetworking_services_projects_global_networks_updateConsumerConfig
fields.description: Service producers use this method to update the configuration of their connection including the import/export of custom routes and subnetwork routes with public IP.

fields.name: servicenetworking_services_projects_global_networks_peeredDnsDomains_create
fields.description: Creates a peered DNS domain which sends requests for records in given namespace originating in the service producer VPC network to the consumer VPC network to be resolved.

fields.name: servicenetworking_services_projects_global_networks_peeredDnsDomains_delete
fields.description: Deletes a peered DNS domain.

fields.name: servicenetworking_services_roles_add
fields.description: Service producers can use this method to add roles in the shared VPC host project. Each role is bound to the provided member. Each role must be selected from within an allowlisted set of roles. Each role is applied at only the granularity specified in the allowlist.

fields.name: servicenetworking_services_dnsZones_add
fields.description: Service producers can use this method to add private DNS zones in the shared producer host project and matching peering zones in the consumer project.

fields.name: servicenetworking_services_dnsZones_remove
fields.description: Service producers can use this method to remove private DNS zones in the shared producer host project and matching peering zones in the consumer project.

fields.name: servicenetworking_services_dnsRecordSets_add
fields.description: Service producers can use this method to add DNS record sets to private DNS zones in the shared producer host project.

fields.name: servicenetworking_services_dnsRecordSets_remove
fields.description: Service producers can use this method to remove DNS record sets from private DNS zones in the shared producer host project.

fields.name: servicenetworking_services_dnsRecordSets_update
fields.description: Service producers can use this method to update DNS record sets from private DNS zones in the shared producer host project.

types.name: DisableVpcServiceControlsRequest_Input
types.description: Request to disable VPC service controls.

inputFields.name: consumerNetwork
inputFields.description: Required. The network that the consumer is using to connect with services. Must be in the form of projects/{project}/global/networks/{network} {project} is a project number, as in '12345' {network} is network name.

types.name: EnableVpcServiceControlsRequest_Input
types.description: Request to enable VPC service controls.

inputFields.name: consumerNetwork
inputFields.description: Required. The network that the consumer is using to connect with services. Must be in the form of projects/{project}/global/networks/{network} {project} is a project number, as in '12345' {network} is network name.

types.name: AddSubnetworkRequest_Input
types.description: Request to create a subnetwork in a previously peered service network.

inputFields.name: consumerNetwork
inputFields.description: Required. The name of the service consumer's VPC network. The network must have an existing private connection that was provisioned through the connections.create method. The name must be in the following format: `projects/{project}/global/networks/{network}`, where {project} is a project number, such as `12345`. {network} is the name of a VPC network in the project.

inputFields.name: subnetwork
inputFields.description: Required. A name for the new subnet. For information about the naming requirements, see [subnetwork](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/reference/rest/v1/subnetworks) in the Compute API documentation.

inputFields.name: region
inputFields.description: Required. The name of a [region](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/regions-zones) for the subnet, such `europe-west1`.

inputFields.name: ipPrefixLength
inputFields.description: Required. The prefix length of the subnet's IP address range. Use CIDR range notation, such as `29` to provision a subnet with an `x.x.x.x/29` CIDR range. The IP address range is drawn from a pool of available ranges in the service consumer's allocated range. GCE disallows subnets with prefix_length > 29

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: Optional. Description of the subnet.

inputFields.name: subnetworkUsers
inputFields.description: A list of members that are granted the `roles/servicenetworking.subnetworkAdmin` role on the subnet.

inputFields.name: consumer
inputFields.description: Required. A resource that represents the service consumer, such as `projects/123456`. The project number can be different from the value in the consumer network parameter. For example, the network might be part of a Shared VPC network. In those cases, Service Networking validates that this resource belongs to that Shared VPC.

inputFields.name: requestedAddress
inputFields.description: Optional. The starting address of a range. The address must be a valid IPv4 address in the x.x.x.x format. This value combined with the IP prefix range is the CIDR range for the subnet. The range must be within the allocated range that is assigned to the private connection. If the CIDR range isn't available, the call fails.

inputFields.name: secondaryIpRangeSpecs
inputFields.description: Optional. A list of secondary IP ranges to be created within the new subnetwork.

inputFields.name: privateIpv6GoogleAccess
inputFields.description: Optional. The private IPv6 google access type for the VMs in this subnet. For information about the access types that can be set using this field, see [subnetwork](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/reference/rest/v1/subnetworks) in the Compute API documentation.

inputFields.name: requestedRanges
inputFields.description: Optional. The name of one or more allocated IP address ranges associated with this private service access connection. If no range names are provided all ranges associated with this connection will be considered. If a CIDR range with the specified IP prefix length is not available within these ranges, the call fails.

inputFields.name: outsideAllocationPublicIpRange
inputFields.description: Optional. Enable outside allocation using public IP addresses. Any public IP range may be specified. If this field is provided, we will not use customer reserved ranges for this primary IP range.

inputFields.name: purpose
inputFields.description: Optional. Defines the purpose field of the subnet, e.g. 'PRIVATE_SERVICE_CONNECT'. For information about the purposes that can be set using this field, see [subnetwork](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/reference/rest/v1/subnetworks) in the Compute API documentation.

inputFields.name: checkServiceNetworkingUsePermission
inputFields.description: Optional. The IAM permission check determines whether the consumer project has 'servicenetworking.services.use' permission or not.

inputFields.name: useCustomComputeIdempotencyWindow
inputFields.description: Optional. Specifies if Service Networking should use a custom time bucket for GCE idempotency. If false, Service Networking uses a 300 second (5 minute) GCE idempotency window. If true, Service Networking uses a custom idempotency window provided by the user in field compute_idempotency_window.

inputFields.name: computeIdempotencyWindow
inputFields.description: Optional. Specifies a custom time bucket for GCE subnetwork request idempotency. If two equivalent concurrent requests are made, GCE will know to ignore the request if it has already been completed or is in progress. Only requests with matching compute_idempotency_window have guaranteed idempotency. Changing this time window between requests results in undefined behavior. Zero (or empty) value with custom_compute_idempotency_window=true specifies no idempotency (i.e. no request ID is provided to GCE). Maximum value of 14 days (enforced by GCE limit).

inputFields.name: allowSubnetCidrRoutesOverlap
inputFields.description: Optional. Defines the allowSubnetCidrRoutesOverlap field of the subnet, e.g. Available in alpha and beta according to [Compute API documentation](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/reference/rest/beta/subnetworks/insert)

inputFields.name: role
inputFields.description: Optional. Defines the role field of the subnet, e.g. 'ACTIVE'. For information about the roles that can be set using this field, see [subnetwork](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/reference/rest/v1/subnetworks) in the Compute API documentation.

inputFields.name: internalRange
inputFields.description: Optional. The url of an Internal Range. Eg: `projects//locations/global/internalRanges/`. If specified, it means that the subnetwork cidr will be created using the combination of requested_address/ip_prefix_length. Note that the subnet cidr has to be within the cidr range of this Internal Range.

inputFields.name: skipRequestedAddressValidation
inputFields.description: Optional. Skips validating if the requested_address is in use by SN VPC’s peering group. Compute Engine will still perform this check and fail the request if the requested_address is in use. Note that Compute Engine does not check for the existence of dynamic routes when performing this check. Caller of this API should make sure that there are no dynamic routes overlapping with the requested_address/prefix_length IP address range otherwise the created subnet could cause misrouting.

inputFields.name: rangeName
inputFields.description: Required. A name for the secondary IP range. The name must be 1-63 characters long, and comply with RFC1035. The name must be unique within the subnetwork.

inputFields.name: ipPrefixLength
inputFields.description: Required. The prefix length of the secondary IP range. Use CIDR range notation, such as `30` to provision a secondary IP range with an `x.x.x.x/30` CIDR range. The IP address range is drawn from a pool of available ranges in the service consumer's allocated range.

inputFields.name: requestedAddress
inputFields.description: Optional. The starting address of a range. The address must be a valid IPv4 address in the x.x.x.x format. This value combined with the IP prefix range is the CIDR range for the secondary IP range. The range must be within the allocated range that is assigned to the private connection. If the CIDR range isn't available, the call fails.

inputFields.name: outsideAllocationPublicIpRange
inputFields.description: Optional. Enable outside allocation using public IP addresses. Any public IP range may be specified. If this field is provided, we will not use customer reserved ranges for this secondary IP range.

types.name: SearchRangeRequest_Input
types.description: Request to search for an unused range within allocated ranges.

inputFields.name: network
inputFields.description: Required. Network name in the consumer project. This network must have been already peered with a shared VPC network using CreateConnection method. Must be in a form 'projects/{project}/global/networks/{network}'. {project} is a project number, as in '12345' {network} is network name.

inputFields.name: ipPrefixLength
inputFields.description: Required. The prefix length of the IP range. Use usual CIDR range notation. For example, '30' to find unused x.x.x.x/30 CIDR range. Actual range will be determined using allocated range for the consumer peered network and returned in the result.

fields.name: isValid
fields.description: Indicates whether all the requested validations passed.

fields.name: existingSubnetworkCandidates
fields.description: List of subnetwork candidates from the request which exist with the `ip_cidr_range`, `secondary_ip_cider_ranges`, and `outside_allocation` fields set.

types.name: mutation_servicenetworking_services_validate_validationError
types.description: The first validation which failed.

types.name: Subnetwork
types.description: Represents a subnet that was created or discovered by a private access management service.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Subnetwork name. See https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/vpc/

fields.name: ipCidrRange
fields.description: Subnetwork CIDR range in `10.x.x.x/y` format.

fields.name: network
fields.description: In the Shared VPC host project, the VPC network that's peered with the consumer network. For example: `projects/1234321/global/networks/host-network`

fields.name: outsideAllocation
fields.description: This is a discovered subnet that is not within the current consumer allocated ranges.

fields.name: secondaryIpRanges
fields.description: List of secondary IP ranges in this subnetwork.

fields.name: region
fields.description: GCP region where the subnetwork is located.

fields.name: rangeName
fields.description: Name of the secondary IP range.

fields.name: ipCidrRange
fields.description: Secondary IP CIDR range in `x.x.x.x/y` format.

inputFields.name: consumerNetwork
inputFields.description: Required. The network that the consumer is using to connect with services. Must be in the form of projects/{project}/global/networks/{network} {project} is a project number, as in '12345' {network} is network name.

inputFields.name: validateNetwork
inputFields.description: The validations will be performed in the order listed in the ValidationError enum. The first failure will return. If a validation is not requested, then the next one will be performed. SERVICE_NETWORKING_NOT_ENABLED and NETWORK_NOT_PEERED checks are performed for all requests where validation is requested. NETWORK_NOT_FOUND and NETWORK_DISCONNECTED checks are done for requests that have validate_network set to true.

inputFields.name: checkServiceNetworkingUsePermission
inputFields.description: Optional. The IAM permission check determines whether the consumer project has 'servicenetworking.services.use' permission or not.

types.name: ConsumerProject_Input
types.description: Represents a consumer project.

inputFields.name: projectNum
inputFields.description: Required. Project number of the consumer that is launching the service instance. It can own the network that is peered with Google or, be a service project in an XPN where the host project has the network.

types.name: RangeReservation_Input
types.description: Represents a range reservation.

inputFields.name: ipPrefixLength
inputFields.description: Required. The size of the desired subnet. Use usual CIDR range notation. For example, '29' to find unused x.x.x.x/29 CIDR range. The goal is to determine if one of the allocated ranges has enough free space for a subnet of the requested size. GCE disallows subnets with prefix_length > 29

inputFields.name: secondaryRangeIpPrefixLengths
inputFields.description: Optional. The size of the desired secondary ranges for the subnet. Use usual CIDR range notation. For example, '29' to find unused x.x.x.x/29 CIDR range. The goal is to determine that the allocated ranges have enough free space for all the requested secondary ranges. GCE disallows subnets with prefix_length > 29

inputFields.name: requestedRanges
inputFields.description: Optional. The name of one or more allocated IP address ranges associated with this private service access connection. If no range names are provided all ranges associated with this connection will be considered. If a CIDR range with the specified IP prefix length is not available within these ranges the validation fails.

inputFields.name: subnetworkCandidates
inputFields.description: Optional. List of subnetwork candidates to validate. The required input fields are `name`, `network`, and `region`. Subnetworks from this list which exist will be returned in the response with the `ip_cidr_range`, `secondary_ip_cider_ranges`, and `outside_allocation` fields set.

types.name: Subnetwork_Input
types.description: Represents a subnet that was created or discovered by a private access management service.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Subnetwork name. See https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/vpc/

inputFields.name: ipCidrRange
inputFields.description: Subnetwork CIDR range in `10.x.x.x/y` format.

inputFields.name: network
inputFields.description: In the Shared VPC host project, the VPC network that's peered with the consumer network. For example: `projects/1234321/global/networks/host-network`

inputFields.name: outsideAllocation
inputFields.description: This is a discovered subnet that is not within the current consumer allocated ranges.

inputFields.name: secondaryIpRanges
inputFields.description: List of secondary IP ranges in this subnetwork.

inputFields.name: region
inputFields.description: GCP region where the subnetwork is located.

inputFields.name: rangeName
inputFields.description: Name of the secondary IP range.

inputFields.name: ipCidrRange
inputFields.description: Secondary IP CIDR range in `x.x.x.x/y` format.

types.name: Connection_Input
types.description: Represents a private connection resource. A private connection is implemented as a VPC Network Peering connection between a service producer's VPC network and a service consumer's VPC network.

inputFields.name: network
inputFields.description: Required. The name of service consumer's VPC network that's connected with service producer network, in the following format: `projects/{project}/global/networks/{network}`. `{project}` is a project number, such as in `12345` that includes the VPC service consumer's VPC network. `{network}` is the name of the service consumer's VPC network.

inputFields.name: reservedPeeringRanges
inputFields.description: The name of one or more allocated IP address ranges for this service producer of type `PEERING`. Note that invoking CreateConnection method with a different range when connection is already established will not modify already provisioned service producer subnetworks. If CreateConnection method is invoked repeatedly to reconnect when peering connection had been disconnected on the consumer side, leaving this field empty will restore previously allocated IP ranges.

inputFields.name: peering
inputFields.description: Output only. The name of the VPC Network Peering connection that was created by the service producer.

inputFields.name: service
inputFields.description: Output only. The name of the peering service that's associated with this connection, in the following format: `services/{service name}`.

types.name: DeleteConnectionRequest_Input
types.description: Request to delete a private service access connection. The call will fail if there are any managed service instances using this connection.

inputFields.name: consumerNetwork
inputFields.description: Required. The network that the consumer is using to connect with services. Must be in the form of projects/{project}/global/networks/{network} {project} is a project number, as in '12345' {network} is a network name.

types.name: UpdateConsumerConfigRequest_Input
types.description: Request to update the configuration of a service networking connection including the import/export of custom routes and subnetwork routes with public IP.

types.name: ConsumerConfig_Input
types.description: Configuration information for a private service access connection.

inputFields.name: producerNetwork
inputFields.description: Output only. The VPC host network that is used to host managed service instances. In the format, projects/{project}/global/networks/{network} where {project} is the project number e.g. '12345' and {network} is the network name.

inputFields.name: consumerImportCustomRoutes
inputFields.description: Import custom routes flag value for peering from consumer to producer.

inputFields.name: consumerExportCustomRoutes
inputFields.description: Export custom routes flag value for peering from consumer to producer.

inputFields.name: producerImportCustomRoutes
inputFields.description: Import custom routes flag value for peering from producer to consumer.

inputFields.name: producerExportCustomRoutes
inputFields.description: Export custom routes flag value for peering from producer to consumer.

inputFields.name: consumerImportSubnetRoutesWithPublicIp
inputFields.description: Import subnet routes with public ip flag value for peering from consumer to producer.

inputFields.name: consumerExportSubnetRoutesWithPublicIp
inputFields.description: Export subnet routes with public ip flag value for peering from consumer to producer.

inputFields.name: producerImportSubnetRoutesWithPublicIp
inputFields.description: Import subnet routes with public ip flag value for peering from producer to consumer.

inputFields.name: producerExportSubnetRoutesWithPublicIp
inputFields.description: Export subnet routes with public ip flag value for peering from producer to consumer.

inputFields.name: reservedRanges
inputFields.description: Output only. The reserved ranges associated with this private service access connection.

inputFields.name: vpcScReferenceArchitectureEnabled
inputFields.description: Output only. Indicates whether the VPC Service Controls reference architecture is configured for the producer VPC host network.

inputFields.name: usedIpRanges
inputFields.description: Output only. The IP ranges already in use by consumer or producer

inputFields.name: cloudsqlConfigs
inputFields.description: Represents one or multiple Cloud SQL configurations.

inputFields.name: consumerPeeringActive
inputFields.description: Output only. If this is true, consumer peering is active.

types.name: GoogleCloudServicenetworkingV1ConsumerConfigReservedRange_Input
types.description: Allocated IP address ranges for this private service access connection.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: The name of the reserved range.

inputFields.name: ipPrefixLength
inputFields.description: The prefix length of the reserved range.

inputFields.name: address
inputFields.description: The starting address of the reserved range. The address must be a valid IPv4 address in the x.x.x.x format. This value combined with the IP prefix length is the CIDR range for the reserved range.

types.name: CloudSQLConfig_Input
types.description: Cloud SQL configuration.

inputFields.name: service
inputFields.description: Required. Peering service used for peering with the Cloud SQL project.

inputFields.name: umbrellaProject
inputFields.description: Required. The project number of the Cloud SQL umbrella project.

inputFields.name: umbrellaNetwork
inputFields.description: Required. The name of the umbrella network in the Cloud SQL umbrella project.

types.name: PeeredDnsDomain_Input
types.description: DNS domain suffix for which requests originating in the producer VPC network are resolved in the associated consumer VPC network.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Required. User assigned name for this resource. Must be unique within the consumer network. The name must be 1-63 characters long, must begin with a letter, end with a letter or digit, and only contain lowercase letters, digits or dashes.

inputFields.name: dnsSuffix
inputFields.description: The DNS domain name suffix e.g. `example.com.`. Cloud DNS requires that a DNS suffix ends with a trailing dot.

types.name: AddRolesRequest_Input
types.description: Request for AddRoles to allow Service Producers to add roles in the shared VPC host project for them to use.

inputFields.name: consumerNetwork
inputFields.description: Required. The network that the consumer is using to connect with services. Must be in the form of projects/{project}/global/networks/{network} {project} is a project number, as in '12345' {network} is a network name.

inputFields.name: policyBinding
inputFields.description: Required. List of policy bindings to add to shared VPC host project.

types.name: PolicyBinding_Input
types.description: Grouping of IAM role and IAM member.

inputFields.name: role
inputFields.description: Required. Role to apply. Only allowlisted roles can be used at the specified granularity. The role must be one of the following: - 'roles/container.hostServiceAgentUser' applied on the shared VPC host project - 'roles/compute.securityAdmin' applied on the shared VPC host project - 'roles/compute.networkAdmin' applied on the shared VPC host project - 'roles/tpu.xpnAgent' applied on the shared VPC host project - 'roles/dns.admin' applied on the shared VPC host project - 'roles/logging.admin' applied on the shared VPC host project - 'roles/monitoring.viewer' applied on the shared VPC host project - 'roles/servicemanagement.quotaViewer' applied on the shared VPC host project

inputFields.name: member
inputFields.description: Required. Member to bind the role with. See /iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/Policy#Binding for how to format each member. Eg. - user:myuser@mydomain.com - serviceAccount:my-service-account@app.gserviceaccount.com

types.name: AddDnsZoneRequest_Input
types.description: Request to add a private managed DNS zone in the shared producer host project and a matching DNS peering zone in the consumer project.

inputFields.name: consumerNetwork
inputFields.description: Required. The network that the consumer is using to connect with services. Must be in the form of projects/{project}/global/networks/{network} {project} is the project number, as in '12345' {network} is the network name.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Required. The name for both the private zone in the shared producer host project and the peering zone in the consumer project. Must be unique within both projects. The name must be 1-63 characters long, must begin with a letter, end with a letter or digit, and only contain lowercase letters, digits or dashes.

inputFields.name: dnsSuffix
inputFields.description: Required. The DNS name suffix for the zones e.g. `example.com.`. Cloud DNS requires that a DNS suffix ends with a trailing dot.

types.name: RemoveDnsZoneRequest_Input
types.description: Request to remove a private managed DNS zone in the shared producer host project and a matching DNS peering zone in the consumer project.

inputFields.name: consumerNetwork
inputFields.description: Required. The network that the consumer is using to connect with services. Must be in the form of projects/{project}/global/networks/{network} {project} is the project number, as in '12345' {network} is the network name.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Required. The name for both the private zone in the shared producer host project and the peering zone in the consumer project.

types.name: AddDnsRecordSetRequest_Input
types.description: Request to add a record set to a private managed DNS zone in the shared producer host project.

inputFields.name: consumerNetwork
inputFields.description: Required. The network that the consumer is using to connect with services. Must be in the form of projects/{project}/global/networks/{network} {project} is the project number, as in '12345' {network} is the network name.

inputFields.name: zone
inputFields.description: Required. The name of the private DNS zone in the shared producer host project to which the record set will be added.

types.name: DnsRecordSet_Input
types.description: Represents a DNS record set resource.

inputFields.name: domain
inputFields.description: Required. The DNS or domain name of the record set, e.g. `test.example.com`. Cloud DNS requires that a DNS suffix ends with a trailing dot.

inputFields.name: type
inputFields.description: Required. The identifier of a supported record type.

inputFields.name: ttl
inputFields.description: Required. The period of time for which this RecordSet can be cached by resolvers.

inputFields.name: data
inputFields.description: Required. As defined in RFC 1035 (section 5) and RFC 1034 (section 3.6.1) for examples see https://cloud.google.com/dns/records/json-record.

types.name: RemoveDnsRecordSetRequest_Input
types.description: Request to remove a record set from a private managed DNS zone in the shared producer host project. The name, type, ttl, and data values must all exactly match an existing record set in the specified zone.

inputFields.name: consumerNetwork
inputFields.description: Required. The network that the consumer is using to connect with services. Must be in the form of projects/{project}/global/networks/{network} {project} is the project number, as in '12345' {network} is the network name.

inputFields.name: zone
inputFields.description: Required. The name of the private DNS zone in the shared producer host project from which the record set will be removed.

types.name: UpdateDnsRecordSetRequest_Input
types.description: Request to update a record set from a private managed DNS zone in the shared producer host project. The name, type, ttl, and data values of the existing record set must all exactly match an existing record set in the specified zone.

inputFields.name: consumerNetwork
inputFields.description: Required. The network that the consumer is using to connect with services. Must be in the form of projects/{project}/global/networks/{network} {project} is the project number, as in '12345' {network} is the network name.

inputFields.name: zone
inputFields.description: Required. The name of the private DNS zone in the shared producer host project from which the record set will be removed.

types.name: String
types.description: The `String` scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.

types.name: Boolean
types.description: The `Boolean` scalar type represents `true` or `false`.

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields`, `interfaces`, and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: NON_NULL
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.name: QUERY
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.

enumValues.name: MUTATION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.

enumValues.name: FIELD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.

enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.

enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.

enumValues.name: SCHEMA
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.

enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.

enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an interface definition.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a union definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM_VALUE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum value definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object type definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object field definition.

directives.name: skip
directives.description: Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: include
directives.description: Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: deprecated
directives.description: Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.

args.name: reason
args.description: Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted using the Markdown syntax, as specified by [CommonMark](https://commonmark.org/).

directives.name: specifiedBy
directives.description: Exposes a URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

args.name: url
args.description: The URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

directives.name: oneOf
directives.description: Indicates exactly one field must be supplied and this field must not be `null`.
